title: paeonia veitchii subsp. altaica
intro: Paeonia veitchii subsp. altaica, also known as the Tibetan peony, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Paeonia genus and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Paeonia veitchii subsp. altaica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through infusion or decoction, and its roots and tubers are also used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and teas.
